title: Articulating endodontic file
abstract: Apparatus and methods for an articulating endodontic file are provided. The apparatus may include two or more file segments. Each file segment may articulate with respect to an adjacent file segment. The articulating file may include a joint between two file segments. The articulating file may include a first joint that allows a file segment to articulate in a first direction. The articulating file may include a second joint that allows a second file segment to articulate in a second direction. The articulating file may be configured such that torque applied to a first file segment is transmitted to one or more other file segments in the file.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An articulating endodontic file comprising: a first segment defining a longitudinal axis and comprising a first rib; a second segment joined to the first segment and comprising a second rib; wherein, in operation: the first rib engages the second rib and transmits force from the first segment to the second segment; and the second segment is configured to deflect relative to the longitudinal axis while receiving the force. 2. The articulating endodontic file of claim 1 further comprising a central member that joins the first segment to the second segment. 3. The articulating endodontic file of claim 1 wherein : the first rib is one of a plurality of first ribs protruding from a distal end of the first segment; and the second rib is one of a plurality of second ribs protruding from a proximal end of the second segment. 4. The articulating endodontic file of claim 3, wherein the plurality of first ribs are spaced about the distal end of the first segment such that each second rib in the plurality of second ribs is configured to fit between two adjacent first ribs in the plurality of first ribs. 5. The articulating endodontic file of claim 1 wherein the force is torque. 6. The articulating endodontic file of claim 3 wherein the first segment is configured to transmit the force to the second segment when at least one rib in the plurality of first ribs interlocks with at least one rib in the plurality of second ribs. 7. The articulating endodontic file of claim 3 wherein, in operation, when the second segment deflects relative to the longitudinal axis of the first segment, at least one rib in the plurality of second ribs interlocks with at least one rib in the plurality of first ribs. 8. The articulating endodontic file of claim 1 further comprising a flexible joint between a distal end of the first segment and a proximal end of the second segment. 9. The articulating endodontic file of claim 8, wherein, in operation, the second segment is configured to deflect relative to the longitudinal axis by bending about the flexible joint. 10. The articulating endodontic file of claim 9, wherein, in operation, the flexible joint provides the second segment a range of motion between 0 and 90 degrees in any direction relative to the longitudinal axis of the first segment . 11. The articulating endodontic file of claim 1 wherein : 

 the first segment comprises at least one cutting edge; and the second segment comprises at least one cutting edge . 12. The articulating endodontic file of claim 11 wherein the second cutting edge is contiguous with the first cutting edge when the longitudinal axis of the first segment is aligned with a longitudinal axis of the second segment. 13. An articulating endodontic file comprising: a first segment comprising a head; and a second segment comprising a recess that is configured to receive the head. 14. The articulating endodontic file of claim 13 further comprising a spacer between the first segment and the second segment. 15. The articulating endodontic file of claim 13 wherein, the head is configured to snap-fit into the recess. 16. The articulating endodontic file of claim 13 further comprising a central member that joins the first segment to the second segment. 17. The articulating endodontic file of claim 16 wherein : the first segment comprises a first inner lumen; the second segment comprises a second inner lumen; and 

 the central member passes through the first inner lumen and passes through the second inner lumen. 18. The articulating endodontic file of claim 17 wherein : the first inner lumen extends through the head; and the second inner lumen extends through the recess. 19. The articulating endodontic file of claim 17 wherein : the first inner lumen comprises a non-circular cross-section; and the central member comprises a cross-section that is configured to engage the non-circular cross-section of the first inner lumen. 20. The articulating endodontic file of claim 19 wherein, in operation, the central member transmits force to the first segment. 21. The articulating endodontic file of claim 20 wherein the force is torque. 22. The articulating endodontic file of claim 13 wherein : the first segment is fluted; and the second segment is fluted. 23. An articulating endodontic file comprising: a central member; a first segment mounted on the central member; and a second segment mounted on the central member. 24. The articulating endodontic file of claim 23: the first segment comprising a first inner lumen; the second segment comprising a second inner lumen; and the central member comprising a non-circular cross-section that is configured to: pass through the first inner lumen and pass through the second inner lumen; and transmit force to the first segment and to the second segment. 25. The articulating endodontic file of claim 23 further comprising a spacer positioned between the first segment and the second segment. 26. The articulating endodontic file of claim 25: the first segment comprising a distal recess configured to receive a proximal end of the spacer; and the second segment comprising a proximal recess configured to receive a distal end of the spacer. 27. The articulating endodontic file of claim 23: the first segment further comprising a first finger extending from a distal end of the first segment; the second segment further comprising a second finger extending from a proximal end of the second segment. 28. The articulating endodontic file of claim 27 further comprising a spacer comprising at least two protrusions defining at least one notch such that the first 

finger or the second finger is configured to be seated in the notch . 29. An articulating endodontic file comprising: a first file segment; a second file segment; a joint linking the first file segment to the second file segment; wherein, in operation, the second file segment rotates about the joint in a plane perpendicular to a longitudinal axis of the first file segment. 30. The articulating endodontic file of claim 29 wherein, the joint is a first joint and the plane is a first plane, the articulating endodontic file further comprising: a third file segment; and a second joint linking the second file segment to the third file segment; wherein, in operation, the third file segment rotates about the second joint in a second plane that is: perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the first file segment; and perpendicular to the first plane. 31. The articulating endodontic file of claim 29, the joint comprising: a proximal extension affixed to a proximal end of the second file segment; a distal cutout in a distal end of the first file segment, the distal cutout configured to receive the proximal extension and defining a first leg and a second leg; and 

 a pin that is configured to pass through the first leg, through the extension and through the second leg, thereby pinning the second file segment to the proximal extension . 32. The articulating endodontic file of claim 30, the second joint comprising: a proximal extension affixed to a proximal end of the third file segment; a pin affixed to the proximal extension and is configured to pass through the second file segment thereby pinning the third file segment to the proximal extension.
